question: Top 5 stores by total sales
sql: SELECT TOP 5 Store, SUM(Sales) AS TotalSales FROM dbo.Train WHERE [Open] = 1 GROUP BY Store ORDER BY TotalSales DESC

question: Average sales by day of week
sql: SELECT DayOfWeek, AVG(Sales) AS AvgSales FROM dbo.Train WHERE [Open] = 1 GROUP BY DayOfWeek ORDER BY DayOfWeek

question: Which day of the week has the highest average sales?
sql: SELECT TOP 1 DayOfWeek, AVG(Sales) AS AvgSales FROM dbo.Train WHERE [Open] = 1 GROUP BY DayOfWeek ORDER BY AvgSales DESC

question: How does promotion affect average sales?
sql: SELECT Promo, AVG(Sales) AS AvgSales, COUNT(*) AS Days FROM dbo.Train WHERE [Open] = 1 GROUP BY Promo ORDER BY Promo

question: Average sales by store type
sql: SELECT s.StoreType, AVG(t.Sales) AS AvgSales FROM dbo.Train t JOIN dbo.Store s ON t.Store = s.Store WHERE t.[Open] = 1 GROUP BY s.StoreType ORDER BY AvgSales DESC

question: Average sales by assortment type
sql: SELECT s.Assortment, AVG(t.Sales) AS AvgSales FROM dbo.Train t JOIN dbo.Store s ON t.Store = s.Store WHERE t.[Open] = 1 GROUP BY s.Assortment ORDER BY AvgSales DESC

question: Monthly total sales trend
sql: SELECT YEAR([Date]) AS Year, MONTH([Date]) AS Month, SUM(Sales) AS TotalSales FROM dbo.Train WHERE [Open] = 1 GROUP BY YEAR([Date]), MONTH([Date]) ORDER BY Year, Month

question: Which stores have the most promo days?
sql: SELECT TOP 10 Store, SUM(Promo) AS PromoDays FROM dbo.Train GROUP BY Store ORDER BY PromoDays DESC

question: Average sales on promo vs non-promo days by store type
sql: SELECT s.StoreType, t.Promo, AVG(t.Sales) AS AvgSales FROM dbo.Train t JOIN dbo.Store s ON t.Store = s.Store WHERE t.[Open] = 1 GROUP BY s.StoreType, t.Promo ORDER BY s.StoreType, t.Promo

question: How many stores are enrolled in Promo2?
sql: SELECT Promo2, COUNT(*) AS StoreCount FROM dbo.Store GROUP BY Promo2

question: Sales on public holidays vs normal days
sql: SELECT StateHoliday, AVG(Sales) AS AvgSales, COUNT(*) AS Days FROM dbo.Train WHERE [Open] = 1 GROUP BY StateHoliday ORDER BY StateHoliday

question: Effect of school holidays on sales
sql: SELECT SchoolHoliday, AVG(Sales) AS AvgSales, COUNT(*) AS Days FROM dbo.Train WHERE [Open] = 1 GROUP BY SchoolHoliday

question: Top 10 stores by average daily sales
sql: SELECT TOP 10 Store, AVG(Sales) AS AvgDailySales FROM dbo.Train WHERE [Open] = 1 GROUP BY Store ORDER BY AvgDailySales DESC

question: Total sales per year
sql: SELECT YEAR([Date]) AS Year, SUM(Sales) AS TotalSales FROM dbo.Train WHERE [Open] = 1 GROUP BY YEAR([Date]) ORDER BY Year

question: How does competition distance relate to average sales?
sql: SELECT CASE WHEN s.CompetitionDistance < 500 THEN 'Under 500m' WHEN s.CompetitionDistance < 2000 THEN '500m-2km' WHEN s.CompetitionDistance < 5000 THEN '2km-5km' ELSE 'Over 5km' END AS DistanceBand, AVG(t.Sales) AS AvgSales, COUNT(DISTINCT t.Store) AS Stores FROM dbo.Train t JOIN dbo.Store s ON t.Store = s.Store WHERE t.[Open] = 1 AND s.CompetitionDistance IS NOT NULL GROUP BY CASE WHEN s.CompetitionDistance < 500 THEN 'Under 500m' WHEN s.CompetitionDistance < 2000 THEN '500m-2km' WHEN s.CompetitionDistance < 5000 THEN '2km-5km' ELSE 'Over 5km' END ORDER BY AvgSales DESC

question: Average sales on weekends vs weekdays
sql: SELECT CASE WHEN DayOfWeek IN (6,7) THEN 'Weekend' ELSE 'Weekday' END AS DayType, AVG(Sales) AS AvgSales FROM dbo.Train WHERE [Open] = 1 GROUP BY CASE WHEN DayOfWeek IN (6,7) THEN 'Weekend' ELSE 'Weekday' END

question: Which month has the highest total sales?
sql: SELECT TOP 1 MONTH([Date]) AS Month, SUM(Sales) AS TotalSales FROM dbo.Train WHERE [Open] = 1 GROUP BY MONTH([Date]) ORDER BY TotalSales DESC

question: Sales trend for store 262 over time
sql: SELECT YEAR([Date]) AS Year, MONTH([Date]) AS Month, SUM(Sales) AS TotalSales FROM dbo.Train WHERE Store = 262 AND [Open] = 1 GROUP BY YEAR([Date]), MONTH([Date]) ORDER BY Year, Month

question: How many days was each store open?
sql: SELECT TOP 20 Store, SUM([Open]) AS OpenDays FROM dbo.Train GROUP BY Store ORDER BY OpenDays DESC

question: Stores with Promo2 and their average sales vs stores without
sql: SELECT s.Promo2, AVG(t.Sales) AS AvgSales, COUNT(DISTINCT t.Store) AS StoreCount FROM dbo.Train t JOIN dbo.Store s ON t.Store = s.Store WHERE t.[Open] = 1 GROUP BY s.Promo2
